skill.name: woocommerce-copy-guidelines
skill.description: Guidelines for writing WooCommerce user interface copy, including sentence case rules for labels, buttons, messages, menus, and headings.
skill.summary: Write consistent WooCommerce UI copy with sentence case rules and practical examples.
skill.author: woocommerce

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by markdown code fences, UI copy examples, and ordinary prose. No evidence found of executable commands, system reconnaissance, weak cryptography use, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False positive external command detections in markdown examples
low_findings.description: The reported backtick findings are fenced text examples for UI labels and messages. They are not shell, Ruby, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside markdown fences labeled text and only contain copywriting examples. There is no command interpreter, script file, or user input execution path.

low_findings.title: False positive system reconnaissance detections in UI text
low_findings.description: The reported lines contain email address validation copy examples. They do not inspect host, user, process, network, or filesystem state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is a messages and notifications section with correct and incorrect capitalization examples. No reconnaissance commands or APIs are present.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptography detections in prose
low_findings.description: The reported SKILL.md lines describe copy guidelines. They do not select cryptographic algorithms, perform hashing, or handle secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is ordinary description text for a writing skill. I found no cryptographic code, configuration, key handling, or security-sensitive data flow.

content.user_title: Write WooCommerce UI copy in sentence case
content.value_statement: WooCommerce UI copy can drift into title case and inconsistent labels. This skill gives sentence case rules, examples, and review prompts for clearer product text.
actual_capabilities: Explains sentence case rules for WooCommerce interface copy.
Shows correct and incorrect examples for buttons, labels, menus, headings, and messages.
Identifies capitalization exceptions for proper nouns, acronyms, and brand names.
Provides quick reference examples for common UI element types.
Supports review and rewriting of short user-facing copy snippets.
limitations: It focuses on copy guidance, not application code or design implementation.
It covers sentence case more than broader brand voice strategy.
It does not include automated linting or repository-wide copy checks.
It provides examples, but final product context still needs human review.

use_cases.title: Review checkout copy
use_cases.description: Check buttons, labels, and messages for sentence case before release.

use_cases.title: Standardize extension settings
use_cases.description: Rewrite payment, shipping, or tax settings labels to match WooCommerce copy patterns.

use_cases.title: Prepare documentation examples
use_cases.description: Create UI text examples that match product capitalization rules.

prompt_templates.title: Check a label
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one UI string
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this WooCommerce UI label for sentence case: [paste label]. Explain the change in one sentence.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite several buttons
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch cleanup for common interface actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite these WooCommerce button labels in sentence case. Keep proper nouns and acronyms unchanged: [paste labels].

prompt_templates.title: Audit a settings screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of related interface copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this WooCommerce settings screen copy for capitalization consistency. Group issues by buttons, labels, headings, and messages: [paste copy].

prompt_templates.title: Create release-ready copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this WooCommerce feature copy for release. Apply sentence case, preserve brand names, and return final text with brief rationale: [paste context].

output_examples.input: Message: Please Enter A Valid Email Address
output_examples.output: Please enter a valid email address

best_practices: Paste the exact UI element type with the copy you want reviewed.
Tell the assistant which words are product names, acronyms, or required brand terms.
Review related strings together so labels, buttons, and messages stay consistent.
anti_patterns: Do not apply title case to multiword buttons or headings.
Do not lowercase official product names, acronyms, or brand names.
Do not use this skill as a replacement for legal, localization, or accessibility review.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps write WooCommerce UI copy in sentence case for labels, buttons, menus, headings, and messages.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it review full product screens?
faq.answer: Yes. Paste the visible copy and ask for a capitalization audit by UI element type.

faq.question: Does it change brand capitalization?
faq.answer: No. It keeps official capitalization for names such as WooCommerce, WordPress, Stripe, and PayPal.

faq.question: Can it enforce copy rules automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and review suggestions, but it does not run automated linting.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files contain markdown guidance and examples, with no executable behavior or data access.
